title: Podman fails to grab docker container and fails when accessing quay.io
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
I am trying to run `haugene/transmission-openvpn:latest-armhf` with podman. The project is originally based on docker. When replacing docker, podman attempts to pull from docker.io and quay.io, quay.io returns a 403 error since the image doesn't exist on quay. 
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run --cap-add=NET_ADMIN -d -v /mnt/data:/data -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro haugene/transmission-openvpn:latest-armhf`

This happens with both `haugene/transmission-openvpn:latest` and `haugene/transmission-openvpn:latest-armhf`


**Describe the results you received:**

```pi@raspberrypi:~ $ podman run --cap-add=NET_ADMIN -d -v /mnt/data:/data -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro haugene/transmission-openvpn:latest-armhf                                                                                                                                   Trying to pull docker.io/haugene/transmission-openvpn:latest-armhf...

comments.body: Are you signed in and have the access right for this image?
Yes, I believe so. When using docker instead of podman it works without issue
@shrub77 You have to login with `podman login quay.io`.

I've encountered this when:

```config
    [registries.search]
    registries = ['docker.io', 'quay.io', 'registry.access.redhat.com']
```

I have logged in with `podman login docker.io` and try to run a `docker.io` container.

The workaround is to remove `quay.io` from the system configuration.

@shrub77 Could you check you have configured `'docker.io'` in `/etc/containers/registries.conf` file? looks like `haugene/transmission-openvpn` does not on quay.io, and when using shortname, podman will try `registries.search` search list.

```yaml
[registries.search]
registries = ['quay.io','docker.io']
```

```
$ ./bin/podman pull haugene/transmission-openvpn:latest-armhf
```

What does `podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map`
Output?
Can you pull this image when run as root?

> DEBU[0001] GET https://auth.docker.io/token?scope=repository%3Ahaugene%2Ftransmission-openvpn%3Apull&service=registry.docker.io

Thanks for checking. Can you install `fuse-overlayfs`? This allows Podman to use the overlay driver even as an ordinary non-root user.

Also, could you retry as root user. The output from https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7501#issuecomment-692918352 (i.e., "manifest unknown") suggests that the image didn't exist. Maybe a typo.
